data.text: a domestic dog; especially: one having the lovable attributes of a puppy
transitory love or affection felt by a child or adolescent
a commercial farming operation in which purebred dogs are raised in large numbers and often in substandard or poor conditions
examples: Currently, hundreds of thousands of dogs languish in puppy mills, large commercial breeding operations that supply most pet stores across the United States and contribute heavily to the pet overpopulation problem.

data.text: cornmeal dough shaped into small balls and fried in deep fat —usually used in plural
having, relating to, or being the relationship between a foster parent and the child who the foster parent cares for
examples: Although both girls lived with Ms. Ayala, Millison was the only one who was officially placed in her care as a foster child.

data.text: being, relating to, or involved in a situation in which temporary care is given to an animal (such as one that is injured or awaiting adoption) in a household or similar setting
examples: Although both girls lived with Ms. Ayala, Millison was the only one who was officially placed in her care as a foster child.
